codeReview.summary: Compared with the original failed feature commit (7b8283190ec252d02f62d6870452992b55c301b1), this fix resolves both prior blockers: sponsor sessions are now tracked in a request_id-keyed map (no single-slot overwrite under concurrent sponsorships), and onboard lifecycle entries now support/emit an ONBOARD badge in runtime event-log plumbing and UI rendering.

issues.description: The VAL-ONBOARD-011 test name claims emission-path coverage ('emits ONBOARD local_mutation entries...') but only type-checks the badge union and a synthetic entry shape; it does not assert absorbDrains completion/failure emission behavior.

sharedStateObservations.observation: runtime-worker procedure reporting is inconsistent here: UI-visible EventLogPanel/CSS changes were made, but no agent-browser step was run and no deviation was recorded.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/runtime-worker/SKILL.md requires agent-browser for user-interactive changes unless the fix is pure non-UI and says omissions must set followedProcedure=false with deviations; fix handoff (3e4d4f13-ba42-49c7-bca2-b2658d018363) records followedProcedure=true with no deviations and verification commands list only tsc/vitest/lint/format. Transcript skeleton query for this worker session contains no agent-browser invocation.

summary: Pass: this fix adequately addresses the original m7-onboard-sponsor-flow failures by isolating concurrent sponsor sessions per request_id and introducing ONBOARD badge support through AppState types, drain ingestion, and EventLogPanel styling/summaries.
